things.name: Norwegian Aviation Museum
things.description: Norway's national aviation museum shaped like a propeller, with historic aircraft, flight simulators and exhibits on Arctic aviation history

things.name: Saltstraumen Maelstrom
things.description: The world's strongest tidal current with 400 million cubic metres of water rushing through a 150-metre strait four times daily, creating powerful whirlpools

things.name: Midnight Sun viewpoints
things.description: Bodo sits above the Arctic Circle; from late May to mid-July the sun never sets, offering extraordinary views of the midnight sun over the fjords

things.name: Bodo Cathedral
things.description: Post-war cathedral rebuilt after WWII bombing in 1956 with striking stained glass by Aage Storstein and a distinctive copper-clad tower

things.name: Nordland Museum
things.description: Regional museum with collections on Sami culture, fishing traditions, coastal life and the dramatic history of Nordland county

things.name: Kjerringoy Trading Post
things.description: Beautifully preserved 19th-century Arctic trading post with original buildings set against dramatic fjord and mountain scenery, a living museum of coastal life

things.name: Rønvik Church
things.description: Historic church with a notable position overlooking the city; one of Bodo's oldest buildings dating from the early 20th century

things.name: Bodo City Centre
things.description: Compact, walkable city centre rebuilt entirely after WWII, with a lively harbour, fish market and views to the Lofoten Islands on clear days

things.name: Beiaren Valley
things.description: Stunning protected wilderness valley south of Bodo with glaciers, waterfalls and some of Norway's most dramatic Arctic mountain scenery

things.name: Aspmyra Stadion
things.description: Home of FK Bodo/Glimt, Norway's dominant club and UEFA Conference League quarter-finalists; known for the devoted Yellow Army supporters
